📈 Elevation profile

Hover the curve for elevation — and a live orange marker shows exactly where you are on the map above (it follows you). Coloured dots = resupply (blue), recommended sleep (purple), cold high passes (red). Two distinct halves: rolling highlands & fjords to ~km 510, then the big mountains (Strynefjellet, Sognefjellet, Valdresflye) through Jotunheimen.

🛏️ Sleep strategy — sleep LOW, sleep SHELTERED.
Even under the midnight sun the high passes sit near freezing at "night" with wind & snow patches — a sleeping-bag bivy up there is a real hypothermia risk. The route drops to warm valley bottoms at the right spots.

★ Primary: Øvre Årdal — km 740, 12 m (sea level). The best bivy on course: warmest air, sheltered fjord town, 24h services, and it sits right after Sognefjellet (hardest climb done) and before Valdresflye (fresh legs for it). At a fast pace you'd arrive ~18–22h in — ideal for a 2–4h sleep.
Sleeping ~2 h per 24 h? You'll need a chain of low, sheltered spots. Use these in order as your fatigue window lands — the pacing planner below auto-places a nap at the best one near each rest mark:
① Sunndalsøra (km 210, sea level) · ② Åndalsnes (km 380, sea level) · ③ Strynedalen (km 510, low valley — Stryn town is a 9 km W detour) · ④ Lom (km 640, 392 m — before Sognefjellet) · ★⑤ Øvre Årdal (km 740, 12 m — the prime spot) · ⑥ Vågåmo / Otta (km 930–940, ~370 m) · ⑦ Alvdal (km 1080, 596 m — last chance).
Golden rule still holds: always take your nap at the low valley town before a big cold pass, never after starting the climb.

Bivy micro-tips: sleep slightly up-slope (cold air & frost pool on the valley floor); use gapahuk lean-tos, rasteplass pull-offs, bus shelters or barn lees; get out of wind & under tree cover; reflective bivy bag over the sleeping bag. Right-to-roam (allemannsretten) applies on uncultivated land >150 m from houses.
⚠️ Cold high-pass danger zones — full kit, do NOT bivy: Sognefjellet (km 692, 1421 m · ~40 km no services) · Valdresflye (km 860, 1390 m) · Grotli/Strynefjellet (km 562, 1137 m) · Dovre/Grimsdalen (km 989, 1170 m, late & tired). 138 km of the route is above 1000 m. Carry food & warm/wind/rain layers into these — the biggest verified on-route gaps are Sunndalsøra→Vistdal (115 km, Aursjøvegen), Dovre→Alvdal (89 km, Dovrefjell), Bøverdalen→Årdal (83 km, Sognefjellet) and Hjelledalen→Bruvoll (72 km, Strynefjellet). See the Resupply section.

⏱️ Pacing plan & resupply timing

Drag the slider (or type a number / tap a preset) to set your target finish time — the whole schedule below recalculates instantly. The model distributes moving time by effort (distance + climbing), then auto-places ~one nap every 24 h at the best low/sheltered sleep spot near each rest mark (💤 rows), plus short resupply stops. It flags whether shops will be open or shut when you arrive — accounting for Norwegian hours (~08:00–22:00 weekdays, shorter Sat) and that groceries are closed all day Sunday (lean on 24h fuel / unmanned supermarkets / small Joker). Set nap length below (default 2 h ≈ your 2 h-per-24 h plan).

72hours target
20h40h60h80h100h

Your 72 h aim: ≈ ~16 km/h overall incl. all stops over 1132 km of gravel with ~18 000 m climbing — a strong, realistic target that leaves room for ~2 h sleep per 24 h and proper resupplies. Drag faster (48–60 h) for a harder push, or slower (90 h) for a steadier ride.
🚫 Day 1 is a Sunday — Norwegian groceries are shut. You start Sun 10:00 and ride all day Sunday, so the big early towns — Tynset (km 30), Oppdal (km 130), Sunndalsøra (km 210), likely Åndalsnes too — are reached with supermarkets closed. On Sunday rely on 24h fuel stations (Circle K/Esso/YX/Uno-X), the race's unmanned supermarkets, and small Joker/Bunnpris <100 m² (some open Sun, esp. tourist spots). Carry food from the start for day 1; stores reopen Monday ~08:00.

🛒 Resupply — verified shops & gaps (OpenStreetMap)

Every shop & fuel station within 3.5 km of the GPX, pulled from OpenStreetMap and matched to route-km. Hours change — re-check before July — but this is the real on-the-ground picture.

⚠️ Three "towns" the route does NOT enter (marker is on the line, but the supermarkets are a detour): Stryn ~9 km W (use Joker Hjelledalen instead) · Folldal ~6.5 km N · Dombås ~10 km W. Don't bank on their shops.

Longest on-route service gaps — nothing open in between, so carry food & water across these:

GapkmLengthLast shop before → first after
Aursjøvegen / Eikesdal205→321115 kmSunndalsøra (full town) → Coop Marked Vistdal
Dovrefjell / Grimsdalen976→106589 kmCoop Prix Dovre (to 21:00) → Circle K Alvdal (24/7)

💡 Clutch shops — open 24h / late / on Sundays (the ones that save you at 03:00 or on day-1 Sunday). All on-route:
  • km 75 · Coop Marked Kvikne — Mo–Sa 06:00–24:00 (closed Sun)
  • km 170 · Matkroken Sunndalsporten — Mo–Su 06:00–24:00 (open Sundays) — last stock-up before the 115 km Aursjøvegen gap
  • km 204 · Sunndalsøra — Kiwi/Rema/Extra to 23:00; Circle K Sun 08–23
  • km 537 · Joker Hjelledalen — Mo–Sa 06:00–24:00 — real last resupply before Strynefjellet (no Stryn detour)
  • km 609 · Coop Prix Bruvoll — 24 hours (Mo–Sa) — first shop after Strynefjellet
  • km 658 · Coop Prix Bøverdalen — 24 hours (Mo–Sa) — last shop before Sognefjellet
  • km 905 · Coop Marked Randsverk — Mo–Su 08:00–20:00 (open Sundays) — in the Jotunheimen crossing
  • km 940 · Circle K E6 Otta — ✅ 24h manned (hot food)
  • km 1066 · Circle K Alvdal — 24/7 — first services after the 89 km Dovrefjell gap
Note: most regular supermarkets close all day Sunday and at 22:00–23:00 otherwise — the shops above are the exceptions.

Detour = rough round-trip extra distance (straight-line estimate; actual road may be longer). Hours from OpenStreetMap.

kmTown/areaTypePlaceOpen (OSM)On route / detour

💧 Water & 🔧 repairs — quick reference

Water = basically a non-issue. Tap water is excellent & free everywhere; mountain streams above the farmland line are safe untreated and you'll cross hundreds. Refill at fuel stations, churches/cemeteries (outdoor taps), rasteplasser and DNT lodges. Carry ~1.5–2 L and top up often; a small filter is backup insurance for lowland/farm sections.

Repairs = be self-sufficient. Real bike shops only exist in the bigger towns (Tynset, Oppdal, Sunndalsøra, Åndalsnes, Stryn, Lom, Beitostølen, Vågå/Otta) and many keep short summer hours / shut at night. The hard middle third (Stryn→Lom→Årdal→Beitostølen) has the fewest options. Carry tubes/plugs, a spare derailleur hanger, quick-links, brake pads & sealant. (Exact shop names/hours not verified here — confirm the ones near your planned arrival times on Google Maps before the start.)

24h resupply: regular groceries run ~08:00–22:00 (shorter Sat) and are closed all day Sunday in Norway — which is your entire day 1. So between ~22:00–08:00, and the whole of Sunday, lean on 24h fuel (Circle K, Esso, YX, Uno-X), the race's unmanned supermarkets (scan bank card to enter Coop/Joker "døgnåpen" stores), and small Joker/Bunnpris <100 m² shops (allowed to open Sundays). Carry enough food from the start to cover day 1. Verified on-route 24h: Circle K E6 Otta (km 940, manned, hot food) & Circle K Alvdal (km 1066, 24/7); plus 24h shops Coop Prix Bøverdalen (km 658) & Bruvoll (km 609), both Mo–Sa (closed Sun). Most other shops shut 22:00–24:00 and all day Sunday — see the Resupply section below.
